rationale: The citing paper uses Zhang, Winterich, and Mittal (2010) as prior evidence that power distance belief has meaningful effects on consumer behavior, helping justify PDB as a key moderator in its framework. It also explicitly reuses the cited paper's sentence-completion manipulation to prime PDB in Study 3. This is more than a passing mention because the citation supports both theoretical positioning and experimental implementation. Therefore the cited paper functions as a substantive basis for the new work.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the cited paper as a key prior foundation for PDB-based consumer behavior effects and borrows its PDB priming method. It then develops a different outcome context (user- vs designer-designed product preference) on top of that base.
